heaviest_rainfall.status: The largest amount of rain ever recorded is on 09 November 2025 when 47.89mm fell at a maximum rate of 24.6mm per hour over the 144.8 hour event.

heaviest_rainrate.status: The rain rate reached 34.3mm per hour on 02 September 2025, and resulted in 21.19mm of rain over 15.0 hours.  This is the highest rate recorded by our weather station.

longest_rain.status: On 09 November 2025, the rain lasted for 144.8 hours, which is the longest time recorded by our database.  47.89mm accumulated and it fell at 24.6mm per hour at times.

most_lightning.status: Since records began on 11 April 2025, the highest lightning strike count in one day is 23 on 07 September 2025.

storm.status: There is a storm in the vicinity.  This is the first storm since 21 December 2025.

top_10_dry.status: It is currently one of the longest dry periods.  It is the ranked 7th since our records started in 23 September 2024.  There have been 9 consecutive days where it has been dry starting on 23 December 2025.  The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 26 November 2025 and lasted for 14 days.

cold_snaps.status: Currently, the weather is stable with no cold snap in progress.  So far, 6 cold snaps have been recorded this year.  The average yearly count is 3.7.

extreme.status: The largest temperature gap recorded is 26.7°C (0.4 - 27.1°C) on 29 April 2025.

heatwaves.status: There is not an active heatwaves.  There has been 6 so far this year, while the average is 1.9.

tropical.status: The most recent tropical night was on 18 July 2025 and this has only occurred 62 times since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is southerly (198°).  In the UK, a southerly wind usually brings milder, moist air from the Atlantic.

gusts.status: The highest wind gust ever seen is 93.7 mph on 13 October 2012.

run.status: To date, the maximum wind run recorded is 1737 miles on 21 October 2012.

speed.status: The largest wind speed ever was on 13 October 2012 with a value of 93.7 mph.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, cooler for the inside temperature (down 8.2°C), outside temperature (down 5.0°C), and feels like temperature (down 4.2°C).

Compared to today, yesterday also had noticeable differences in cloud levels (up 281 m).

The past week and month had observable variation in dew point temperature (up 8.8°C), outside temperature (up 9.0°C), cloud levels (up 320 m), and inside humidity (up 7%) when compared to today.

Today’s average cloud levels is 281.2m lower than yesterday, 320.2m lower than the past week, 37.0m higher than the past month.

The cloud levels for yesterday ranged from 828m to 2280m and averaged 999.7m, while the past week and month indicated warmer conditions.

summary.summary: Inside; a mild and comfortable temperature is anticipated. Outside; subzero temperatures are forecasted. Also, dry and less humid conditions and near calm conditions. A few light raindrops may fall, occasional overcast conditions, and bright intervals likely. High pressure suggests settled weather.

washing.phrase: The current temperature is too low (-5.7°C) for optimal drying conditions.  Any drying will take longer.
